name: DoD SkillBridge Program
whatItProvides: Civilian job training, apprenticeships, or internships for active-duty service members during their final 180 days of service, with continued military pay and benefits.

name: Army Career Skills Program (CSP)
whatItProvides: Provides vocational training and internships on or near installations for transitioning Soldiers in their final 180 days of service.

name: Boots to Business (SBA)
whatItProvides: Entrepreneurial training track introducing transitioning service members and spouses to business plan development and startup models.

name: DOL Employment Navigator & Partnership Program (ENPP)
whatItProvides: Individualized, one-on-one career assistance including resume matching, digital networking strategies, and partner referrals.

name: DOL TEAMS Spouse Workshops
whatItProvides: Employment assistance workshops specifically targeted at military spouses and caregivers, providing networking and job-hunting tips.

name: Career and Credential Exploration (C2E)
whatItProvides: Vocational and technical credential exploration track focused on identifying licensing and accreditation opportunities.

name: DOL Employment Workshop
whatItProvides: A deep-dive employment workshop for job searches, drafting tailored civilian resumes, mock interviews, and negotiating pay.

name: TAP Education Track
whatItProvides: A two-day elective track for service members planning to attend a college or university, guiding them through admission and application processes.

name: TAP-to-VR&E Bridge
whatItProvides: Allows service members with an expected disability rating to apply early for Chapter 31 VR&E services to bridge the transition to employment.

name: TAP-to-GI Bill Bridge
whatItProvides: Pre-separation planning to utilize the Post-9/11 GI Bill or Montgomery GI Bill for degrees, flight schools, and trade programs.

name: VET TEC 2.0 Bridge
whatItProvides: Programs designed to connect transitioning members with high-tech training providers (coding, IT security) if eligibility standards are met.

name: COOL / Credentialing Assistance
whatItProvides: Branch-specific programs (Army COOL, Navy COOL, Marine COOL, AF COOL) funding civilian credential exams and coursework before separation.
